---
title: "Fields & Validation"
---

## Auto Formatting Of Valid Currency Amounts

The following formatting is automatically applied to valid currency amounts:

-
.00 is appended to whole number (no decimal point/value) values.

-
Commas are embedded between the "thousands" values, as shown in the following examples:

-
1234.56 is auto-formatted as 1,234.56.

-
123456.78 is auto-formatted as 123,456.78.

-
1234567.89 is auto-formatted as 1,234,567.89.

-
12345678.90 is auto-formatted as 12,345,678.90.

Note:
Properly entering a value for cents is accepted as valid (for example, entering .50 for 50 cents).

## Bar Code Extraction Arbitration Logic

Documents can be indexed based on bar code values. Bar codes are filtered by type and region to determine the bar code which needs to be extracted.

When extracting bar code data for a Document Class during indexing, multiple bar codes with different values may match the specifications configured for indexing values. These bar codes may be present either on the same page or on multiple pages within a document.

The Hyland Experience Capture application uses the following arbitration methods to resolve any conflict arising from multiple valid bar codes when indexing data.

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Filter Out Data Types | This removes all the bar code values that are not valid for the field that is to be populated. For example, if the field is a numeric field, bar code values that do not meet the field data type definition for numeric are removed. |
| Go with the first page with a matching bar code | If there are multiple pages with valid bar code values, the bar code on the first page that matches the extraction criteria is considered for extraction. |
| Top-most Bar code | If there are multiple bar codes with valid values on the chosen page, the top-most bar code is considered for extraction. |
| Left-most Bar code | If there are multiple bar codes that are considered to be the top-most on the page (according to the top edge of the bar code), the bar code placed at the left-most corner of the page is considered for extraction. |

## Currency Handling And Validation

While performing extraction verification of documents with a currency field, any number entered in the currency amount field is auto-formatted according to the validation rules in the following sections.

When considering currency validation, also note the following:

-
Only U.S. currency formats are supported.

-
Whole numbers (without commas or decimals) are always valid. When these values are exported from the Hyland Capture application, .00 is automatically appended to them.

## Date Handling And Validation

The following criteria must be followed while performing extraction verification of documents with a date field.

Automated date conversion: For numeric dates either entered, or pasted from the document (using rubber banding or selecting), you need to convert all dates into the MM/DD/YYYY format. The acceptable input formats with segmentation characters being slash (/), period (.), dash (-), are as follows:

-
M/D/YY, MM/D/YY, MM/DD/YY, MM/DD/YYYY

-
YY/M/D, YY/M/DD, YY/MM/DDYYYY/MM/DD

-
D/M/YY, DD/M/YY, DD/MM/YY, DD/MM/YYYY

-
Ambiguous dates, where it could be MM/DD or DD/MM need to be assumed as being MM/DD.

The acceptable input formats without any segmentation characters are as follows:

-
A minimum of 6 digits is required

-
MMDDYY, MMDDYYYY

-
DDMMYY, DDMMYYYY

-
YYMMDD, YYYYMMDD

-
Ambiguous dates, where it could be MMDD or DDMM need to be assumed as being MMDD.

## Decimal Point Validation

Consider the following validation rules for decimal points:

-
Values with more than two characters after the decimal point are flagged as INVALID (for example, 12.456). The reason for this is that during OCR, if the comma (thousands separator) is misread as a period, the incorrect value would be marked as valid. Consider the following examples:

-
If the value on the document is 12,456 and during OCR the comma is misread as a period (12.456), it is getting converted to 12.46 when it should be 12,456.

-
If the value on the document is 12,456,000 and during OCR the last comma is misread as a period (12,456.000), it is getting converted to 12,456.00 when it should be 12,456,000.

-
Values with only one character after the decimal point are flagged as INVALID. For example, if the value on the document is 12,456.71 but OCR captures 12,456.7, converting it to 12,456.70 makes it an invalid assumption.

-
Values with more than one decimal point are flagged as INVALID (for example, 12.456.00).

## Duplicating A Bar Code Profile

You can duplicate a bar code profile and create its copy. The duplicated profile contains all the information as the original profile, except for the content queue and class association details.

To duplicate a bar code profile:

1.  From the Hyland Experience Capture Administration user interface, click the Bar Code Profiles tab. The Bar Code Profiles screen is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Click the Duplicate icon next to the bar code profile you want to duplicate.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  In the Duplicate bar code profile dialog box, rename the bar code profile name, if required.
4.  Click Save.

## List Handling And Validation

While performing extraction verification of documents with a list field, you can type to search for a list item, but you must select a value from the items available in the drop-down list.

Consider the following validation rules for list fields:

- After a list value is specified and the batch is submitted, the list value remains unchanged, even if the list is later modified in the Administration interface.
- If a batch contains a list field that has already been indexed, but the list has been modified in the Administration interface in such a way that the indexed value is no longer available, a new value must be specified for the list field. You can select a new value only by reopening the batch.
- If a batch contains a list field that has already been indexed, but the field has been modified in the Administration interface in such a way that the list has been deleted, the list field reverts to a normal field and the previously indexed value remains as its specified value.

## Metadata Handling And Validation

When indexing documents in the Metadata Panel, you must ensure all entered values are valid for their fields' metadata types before you can submit the batch for export. In some cases, entered values are automatically converted to the proper format for the field's type. However, if an entered value is flagged as invalid, an error message is displayed in red text below the field.

Note:
All metadata fields are limited to 1,024 characters. If metadata is exported from the Hyland Capture application and imported into a repository with more restrictive character limits, any metadata values exceeding the character limits set for the repository are truncated on import.

For more information on how specific metadata types are handled and validated, see the following sections:

-
Date Handling and Validation

-
Currency Handling and Validation

-  List Handling and Validation

## Validations

## Comma Validation

Consider the following validation rules for commas:

-
Values with fewer than three numbers after a comma are flagged as INVALID (for example, 123,45).

## Other Validation

Consider the following validation rule:

-
Values with a 0 as the first, leftmost digit are flagged as INVALID. This could indicate that a leading digit was missed via OCR or other means. For example, 000,234.56 is not assumed to be $234.56, as the leading digit could have actually been a 6 that was misread via OCR (making the correct value $600,234.56 in that case).

## Supported Bar Code Types

The following bar code types are supported in the Hyland Capture application:

-
Aztec

-
Codabar

-
Code 128

-
Code 39 Extended

-
Code 93 Extended

-
Data Matrix

-
IATA 2 of 5

-
Interleaved 2 of 5

-
Matrix 2 of 5

-
Patch 2

-
Patch T

-
PDF417

-
QR code

## Viewing The Bar Code Profiles List

The Bar Code Profiles screen displays a list of all the available bar code profiles.

The following columns are displayed on the Bar Code Profiles screen. Each column provides some bar code specific information.

![The Bar code Profiles list](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
-
-

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Bar Code Profile Name | The name of the bar code profile. |
| Bar Code Type and Value | The bar code type associated with this profile and its value. |
| Used in | Displays whether the bar code profile is being used for bar code classification, extraction, or, separation. Each status is color coded. Orange indicates classification. Green indicates extraction. Blue indicates separation. |
|  | Duplicate bar code profile. For more information on duplicating a bar code profile, see Duplicating a bar code profile. |

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The following table explains the different pagination elements on the Bar Code Profiles screen.

| Pagination Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Go to box | Type the number of the page where you want to navigate and press Enter. |
| Page numbers | The first, the current, and the last page numbers are displayed. The current page number is highlighted with a blue underline. You can click a page number to navigate to that particular page. You can also click < or > to move back or forward a page. |
| Batches per page | From the batches per page list, select the number of batches you want to view on the Bar Code Profiles page. You can select from 10, 25, 50, or 100. |

## Working With The Metadata Panel

The Metadata Panel allows you to perform the following activities:

-
View the metadata fields for each document within the selected batch.

Note:
It is recommended that you use the Enter key to navigate through the fields and validate them.

-
Enter data for each metadata field, that has no extracted data.

-
Verify the metadata for each field, that has a low confidence extraction value, indicated by the yellow eye icon. For more information, see Verifying Document Metadata.
